name: Chuck Faranda Galaxy Workflow
description: Broadband galaxy workflow optimized for preserving dust lanes, natural star colors, and fine spiral detail.

steps.note: Crop only stacking artifacts. Preserve galaxy halo and tidal streams whenever possible.
Remove pedestal without clipping the black point.
Preferred: AutoBGE. Alternatives: GraXpert AI or VeraLux Nox. Use conservative settings to preserve faint galaxy halos and IFN.
Neutralize background after gradient removal. Verify background remains neutral.
Preferred: SPCC. Confirm realistic stellar populations and galaxy core color.
RC-Astro BlurXTerminator. Preferred: BlurXTerminator (moderate, ~20–30%), then NoiseXTerminator (light). StarXTerminator is optional. Alternatives: SyQon Parallax and SyQon Prism.
RC-Astro NoiseXTerminator. Apply conservatively for the target.
RC-Astro StarXTerminator. Use only when the workflow calls for starless processing.
Preferred: Generalized Hyperbolic Stretch. Stretch gradually to protect bright galaxy cores.
Increase contrast gently. Avoid oversaturating the core or clipping faint outer arms.
Optional. Use lightly to enhance dust lanes only.
Archive a 16-bit TIFF before producing web images.

author: Chuck Faranda
